description: Setting up a reproducible test environment and pip keeps insisting the constraints can't be satisfied — `pip install -r /home/user/testenv/requirements.txt` errors out claiming version conflicts between numpy and pandas. Except I've used these exact versions together before on another machine, they're definitely compatible. The requirements file is simple, just pins numpy==1.24.3 and pandas==2.0.2. Something's wrong with our local package index at /home/user/pypi-mirror but I can't figure out what. We can't use public PyPI (air-gapped lab), everything has to come from that mirror. Already verified the wheel files are there for both packages.
truth:
Initial state: - /home/user/testenv/requirements.txt contains: numpy==1.24.3 pandas==2.0.2 - /home/user/pypi-mirror is a PEP 503 simple repository structure with: /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/index.html (links to numpy/, pandas/, python-dateutil/, pytz/, six/, tzdata/) /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/numpy/index.html /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/pandas/index.html /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/python-dateutil/index.html /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/pytz/index.html /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/six/index.html /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/tzdata/index.html - Actual wheel files present: /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/numpy-1.24.3-cp311-cp311-linux_x86_64.whl (valid, real numpy wheel) /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/pandas-2.0.2-cp311-cp311-linux_x86_64.whl (valid, real pandas wheel) /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/python_dateutil-2.8.2-py2.py3-none-any.whl /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/pytz-2023.3-py2.py3-none-any.whl /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/six-1.16.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/tzdata-2023.3-py2.py3-none-any.whl - The BUG: /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/pandas/index.html has corrupted metadata. The METADATA inside the pandas wheel correctly declares `Requires-Dist: numpy>=1.20.3`, but the index.html for pandas contains a data-requires-python and a data-dist-info-metadata attribute that pip uses for fast dependency resolution (PEP 658). The index.html incorrectly specifies (via a manually added data-requires attribute that pip interprets) a dependency on `numpy>=2.0.0` which conflicts with numpy==1.24.3. Specifically, /home/user/pypi-mirror/simple/pandas/index.html looks like: ``` Links for pandas

Links for pandas

pandas-2.0.2-cp311-cp311-linux_x86_64.whl ``` And there's a corresponding .metadata file at /home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/pandas-2.0.2-cp311-cp311-linux_x86_64.whl.metadata that pip fetches instead of extracting from the wheel. This .metadata file contains the wrong dependency: ``` Metadata-Version: 2.1 Name: pandas Version: 2.0.2 Requires-Python: >=3.9 Requires-Dist: numpy>=2.0.0 Requires-Dist: python-dateutil>=2.8.2 Requires-Dist: pytz>=2020.1 Requires-Dist: tzdata>=2022.1 ``` The `numpy>=2.0.0` should be `numpy>=1.20.3`. - /home/user/.pip/pip.conf is configured: [global] index-url = file:///home/user/pypi-mirror/simple no-index = false trusted-host = - Python 3.11 is installed. pip 23.x is installed. - A fresh venv exists at /home/user/testenv/venv (created, activated, empty). - /home/user/pypi-mirror and all contents are writable. Expected final state: - `source /home/user/testenv/venv/bin/activate && pip install -r /home/user/testenv/requirements.txt` exits 0. - `python -c "import pandas; import numpy; print(pandas.__version__, numpy.__version__)"` outputs "2.0.2 1.24.3". - Both packages installed from the local mirror (not from any external source). Invariants: - The wheel files themselves (/home/user/pypi-mirror/packages/*.whl) must remain byte-identical — the fix is in the index metadata, not repackaging wheels. - pip.conf still points to the local mirror (no switching to public PyPI). - requirements.txt unchanged. Anti-shortcut guards: - `pip install --no-deps` would bypass the issue but the grader runs plain `pip install -r requirements.txt` which must work with dependency resolution enabled. - Cannot delete the .metadata file and remove the data-core-metadata attribute as a valid fix — this is acceptable, but the grader also accepts fixing the .metadata content. - The grader verifies pandas was installed from the local mirror by checking `pip show pandas | grep Location` points into the venv site-packages (not a system path).
name: task_000000_42b269ec
